children.code:
def CalculateTotal(items: list, products_data: list) -> float:
    total = 0.0
    for item, product in zip(items, products_data):
        price = product.get('price', 0)
        quantity = item.get('quantity', 0)
        total += price * quantity
    return total

def RestoreSingleItemStock(item: dict) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
    product_id = item.get('product_id')
    quantity = item.get('quantity')
    if product_id is None or quantity is None:
        return False, "Missing 'product_id' or 'quantity' in item"
    product = get_product(product_id)
    if product is None:
        return False, f"Product with id {product_id} not found"
    current_stock = product['stock']
    new_stock = current_stock + quantity
    if new_stock < 0:
        return False, "Resulting stock cannot be negative"
    update_product(product_id, {'stock': new_stock})
    return True, ""

decomposition_rationale: The parent function manage_order receives a command and dispatches to one of eight child functions based on the command field. Each child handles exactly one command, performing the necessary validations and data operations. The children cover all functional requirements: CreateOrder (FR-001), PayOrder (FR-002), ShipOrder (FR-003), CompleteOrder (FR-004), CancelOrder (FR-005), ListOrders (FR-006), GetUserOrders (FR-007), ListProducts (FR-008). The parent coordinates by parsing the command and calling the appropriate child, then returning the result. Global variables are distributed: CreateOrder uses users (read), products (read_write), orders (write); PayOrder uses orders (read_write) and users (read_write); ShipOrder and CompleteOrder use orders (read_write); CancelOrder uses orders (read_write), products (read_write), users (read_write); ListOrders and GetUserOrders use orders (read); ListProducts uses products (read). All children use the provided data interfaces.

subprd.purpose: Order Management System
subprd.description:
INPUT FORMAT:
  Format: json
  Description: Input is a JSON object passed to the manage_order function. The command field specifies the operation, and order_data contains parameters.
  Schema:
    command: string (one of: create_order, pay_order, ship_order, complete_order, cancel_order, list_orders, get_user_orders, list_products)
    order_data: {'user_id': 'int (optional)', 'order_id': 'int (optional)', 'items': 'list of objects with product_id (int) and quantity (int) (optional)', 'status_filter': 'string (optional)', 'user_filter': 'int (optional)', 'low_stock': 'bool (optional)'}
  Examples:
    {"command": "create_order", "order_data": {"user_id": 1, "items": [{"product_id": 2, "quantity": 2}]}}
    {"command": "pay_order", "order_data": {"order_id": 1}}
    {"command": "list_products", "order_data": {"low_stock": true}}

OUTPUT FORMAT:
  Format: json
  Description: Output is a JSON object with success flag, message, and optional data.
  Schema:
    success: bool
    message: string
    data: object (optional, varies by command)
  Examples:
    {"success": true, "message": "\u8ba2\u5355\u521b\u5efa\u6210\u529f", "data": {"order_id": 1, "total_price": 199.8}}
    {"success": false, "message": "\u5546\u54c1\u5e93\u5b58\u4e0d\u8db3", "data": {"product_id": 1, "available": 10, "requested": 15}}

Functional Requirements:
[FR-001] Create Order: User creates an order with user ID and item list. Check user existence, product stock, calculate total price, deduct stock, create order record with status pending.
[FR-002] Pay Order: User pays for an order. Check order exists and status is pending, check user balance, deduct balance, update order status to paid.
[FR-003] Ship Order: Ship an order. Check order exists and status is paid, update status to shipped.
[FR-004] Complete Order: Complete an order. Check order exists and status is shipped, update status to completed.
[FR-005] Cancel Order: Cancel an order. Check order exists and status is pending or paid, restore product stock, refund balance if paid, update status to cancelled.
[FR-006] List Orders: List orders with optional filters by user and status. Display order details including items, total price, status, and creation time.
[FR-007] Get User Orders: Get all orders for a specific user. Calculate total spent and count of orders per status.
[FR-008] List Products: List all products with info and stock. Optionally filter low-stock products.

CancelOrder.behavior: Retrieves order via orders.get, checks status is pending or paid, retrieves order items, restores stock via products.update, if status was paid refunds balance via users.update, updates order status to cancelled via orders.update, returns success or error.
postconditions: If success, order status is cancelled; stock restored; balance refunded if paid

ListOrders.purpose: Handle list_orders command: list orders with optional filters by user and status.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success response with list of orders.

ListOrders.behavior: Calls orders.list with filters from order_data (status_filter, user_filter), returns success with data containing orders list.
postconditions: Returns list of orders matching filters

GetUserOrders.purpose: Handle get_user_orders command: get all orders for a specific user, calculate total spent and count per status.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success response with orders, total_spent, and status_counts.

GetUserOrders.behavior: Calls orders.list with filter user_id=order_data['user_id'], iterates orders to compute total_spent (sum of total_price for paid/shipped/completed) and status_counts (dict of status to count), returns success with data containing orders, total_spent, status_counts.
postconditions: Returns orders list, total_spent, and status_counts

ListProducts.purpose: Handle list_products command: list all products with optional low-stock filter.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success response with list of products.

ListProducts.behavior: Calls products.list to get all products, if order_data.get('low_stock') is True, filters products where stock < 10 (or some threshold), returns success with data containing products list.
postconditions: Returns list of products, optionally filtered by low stock

attempt_history.decomposition_rationale: The parent function manage_order receives a command and dispatches to one of eight child functions based on the command field. Each child handles exactly one command, performing the necessary validations and data operations. The children cover all functional requirements: CreateOrder (FR-001), PayOrder (FR-002), ShipOrder (FR-003), CompleteOrder (FR-004), CancelOrder (FR-005), ListOrders (FR-006), GetUserOrders (FR-007), ListProducts (FR-008). The parent coordinates by parsing the command and calling the appropriate child, then returning the result. Global variables are distributed: CreateOrder uses users (read), products (read_write), orders (write); PayOrder uses orders (read_write) and users (read_write); ShipOrder and CompleteOrder use orders (read_write); CancelOrder uses orders (read_write), products (read_write), users (read_write); ListOrders and GetUserOrders use orders (read); ListProducts uses products (read). All children use the provided data interfaces.
